text.title: openapi-specification Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for openapi-specification as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLM duplicates schemas instead of using $ref for existing components.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Duplicate schemas produce structurally identical definitions that can diverge on the next edit. Run a post-pass that detects structurally equal schemas without a shared $ref and flags them for consolidation.

failure-mode.description: Agent emits type: object without a required array.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Generators treat all properties as Partial. TypeScript clients compile but fail at runtime on missing fields. Every object schema must have an explicit required list, even if it mirrors all properties.

failure-mode.description: Using oneOf for union types without a discriminator.propertyName.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a discriminator, many generators silently produce any for the union type, defeating the purpose of the union. Verify the generated client output, not just spec validity.

failure-mode.description: Agent re-indents the entire YAML file on update, producing a giant unreadable diff.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: YAML formatting changes obscure the semantic diff and trigger false positives in oasdiff. Pin to a formatter (prettier --parser yaml or yq) and apply it in a separate commit before the semantic change.

failure-mode.description: Missing security block on POST /login or similar public-entry endpoints.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without security: [], the global security scheme is inherited, making the login endpoint appear to require authentication before issuing credentials. Add a Spectral rule that fails if security is missing on any operation.

failure-mode.description: Using x-* custom extensions that are not supported by the target generator.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Custom extensions are silently ignored by most generators and do not reach the generated client. Only use x-* extensions documented by the specific generator you are targeting.
